events.title: ūüĆą WICKED
events.description:
This Pride Month, we're leaning into stories that feel like ours, and WICKED tells one of the most resonant ones on Broadway: what happens when the world misunderstands you, labels you, and tries to define who you are.

WICKED has been a love letter to our community long before Broadway said so, and this June, we're making it easier for you to experience it live. IMPAQT GSO is sharing an exclusive Pride Month presale for the Tanger Center's upcoming production, right here in Greensboro.

Welcome to the wonderful Land of Oz‚Ä¶ where there is a young woman, born with emerald-green skin‚ÄĒsmart, fiery, misunderstood and possessing an extraordinary talent. When she meets a bubbly blonde who is exceptionally popular, their initial rivalry turns into the unlikeliest of friendships‚Ä¶until the world decides to call one good and the other one wicked. With a thrilling score that includes the hits "Defying Gravity," "Popular" and "For Good," WICKED has been called "A magical Broadway musical with brains, heart and courage" (Time Magazine).

Dates: November 18 ‚Äď December 6
Best availability remains for the Nov. 25 ‚Äď Dec. 6 performances

Use code 08GREEN at checkout for presale pricing. Valid through Wednesday, June 17 at 10pm, so don't wait.

Pride Month gave us the reminder. Now go get your tickets.

Link in the comments.

 #GreensboroPride #Pride #IMPAQTGSO #greensboro

events.title: ūüĆą Wrapped in Pride: An Immersive Evening of Art, Fashion & Queer Expression
events.description:
Greensboro, this one is special, and we need you to understand why.

Wrapped in Pride: An Immersive Evening of Art, Fashion & Queer Expression is happening June 23rd from 6‚Äď10PM at 313 E Market Street. But this is more than a Pride event. This is the first time D Scents Candles & More is opening up this space to the public, and we are honored to be part of that moment.

DeAngelo, the founder of D Scents, built this brand on intention, growth, and the belief that people deserve beauty and peace in their everyday lives. What started as a candle company has grown into something far greater, a space rooted in purpose, and a home for community.

Wrapped in Pride is how we celebrate that. Together.

Here is what the night holds:

Original visual art from 4 local LGBTQIA+ artists
A fashion installation featuring a designer presentation and immersive window display
Live performances from Drag Queens, Drag Kings, and Bearded Queens
Curated music, cocktails, and small bites

Admission is free. This night is a gift, from DeAngelo to the community, and from IMPAQT GSO to Greensboro. Come ready to be moved, to connect, and to witness something being built in real time.

And tip your performers. They pour everything into this for you.

Tag someone who needs to be in that room on June 23rd.

#IMPAQTGSO #PrideMonth2026 #GreensboroPride #GreensboroNC #Pride

events.title: ūüĆą SIX The Musical
events.location: Steven Tanger Center for the Performing Arts
events.description:
It‚Äôs time to take back the microphone and reclaim your crown!

We are SO excited to give away 4 tickets to the global sensation SIX for the opening night performance on Tuesday, May 5th, at the Tanger Center!

From Tudor Queens to Pop Icons, the SIX wives of Henry VIII remix five hundred years of historical heartbreak into a euphoric celebration of 21st-century power. The New York Times says SIX ‚ÄúTOTALLY RULES!‚ÄĚ and The Washington Post hails it as ‚Äúexactly the kind of energizing, inspirational illumination this town aches for!"

To enter: Tell us about a moment you felt fierce, stood up for yourself, or took back your narrative. We want to hear those "reclaim your crown" moments!

Two winners will be chosen to snag 2 tickets each for the May 5th show.

Show Details: Steven Tanger Center for the Performing Arts Tuesday, May 5th (Show runs May 5-10).

 If you can't make the opening night, you can get tickets from the link in the comments.

We can‚Äôt wait to read your stories below. Let‚Äôs show them how Greensboro shines!

#SIXTheMusical #GreensboroPride #IMPAQTGSO #TangerCenter #ReclaimYourCrown

events.title: ūüé® GPS Student Residency Program | Summer Studios: Arts on Site
events.description:
MEET MACY ‚ú® Current Artist in Residence, as part of the GPS Student Residency Program | Summer Studios: Arts on Site! ‚ú®

About Macy macyjart 
Macy James (BFA Sculpture & Ceramics) will explore various materials that she has yet to try, though has long wanted to. Within this residency, Macy plans to work with more accessible material specifically, in addition to those she is accustomed to, like found and second-hand goods sourced from local places - materials that do not require large shops or expensive tools. Another material interest is found wood, like driftwood, to experiment with wood carving or wood burning alongside bead and wire sculptures, which she presently has no experience working with. The hope is that this will help her develop a healthy habit of creating art with widely accessible materials and make her an overall more multifaceted artist.

About the Student Residency Program:
Designed to support UNCG undergraduate student artists (BA + BFA) striving to develop, adapt, and/or reinvent their creative process and to promote artistic growth and development, artists are afforded two-weeks of uninterrupted research and development, coupled with financial support and public presentational platforms to share their creative work. 

Join our AIRs in their studio practices ūüďÖ:
May 11-22, 2026

Public Open Studio Hours:
Monday, May 18th | 12-3P
Wednesday, May 20th | 2-5P

Closing Reception + Open Studio: 
Friday, May 22nd | 6-8P

Learn more by clicking ‚ÄúHappening Now‚ÄĚ in the Linktree above in our profile

events.title: ūüé® Homebody
events.description:
Thank you to everyone who joined us for the reception of Homebody by Kathryn Hood!ūü•ā

The exhibition is still open until 5pm ‚ÄĒ don‚Äôt miss out on experiencing Homebody

************************
NOW OPEN @ GPS
Homebody

MFA Thesis Exhibition
by Kathryn Hood

Exhibition: April 28 - May 2, 2026
Reception: Friday, May 1st, 6-8p

Gallery Open Hours:
Tuesday - Friday 12-5p
Saturday, 2-5p

Homebody is an exhibition of collages and sculptures in which Kathryn Hood carves her perceptions of tense muscles into paper and upholstery foam. She embraces the body-mind connection, an essential tool for self-understanding, as she houses ungrounded avatars within handmade paper and muscular furnishings outside of formal traditions. Hood composes the interior feelings of ideas & emotions with the exterior feeling of tension. This duality of perception reflects the disparate nature of themes explored, such as femininity, domesticity, and the performance of identity.

Homebody is the residence of textured perceptions. This space is an act of resensitization.

Learn more by clicking ‚ÄúHappening Now‚ÄĚ in the Linktree above in our profile

events.title: ūüé® Garden of Corporeal Fantasy
events.description:
Thank you to everyone who joined us in celebrating the reception Garden of Corporeal Fantasy by Victoria Mercado-Lues

The exhibition is still open until 5pm ‚ÄĒ step inside before it closes

************************
NOW OPEN @ GPS
Garden of Corporeal Fantasy
MFA Thesis Exhibition
by Victoria Mercado-Lues

Exhibition: April 21 - 25, 2026
Reception: Friday, April 24th, 6-8p

Gallery Open Hours:
Tuesday - Friday 12-5p
Saturday, 2-5p

Brightly saturated and fantastical, Garden of Corporeal Fantasy explores the meeting of nature and flesh in oil paintings and ceramics. With invented creatures and visceral biological imagery, the works reframe the gaze towards the bodily through a lens of wonder ‚ÄĒ a less punitive lens than the centuries-long and still recurring traditions of bodies as commodified, objectified, or sinful.

This exhibition addresses our discomfort surrounding the body by combining it with the godly imposing presence of the landscape, like waterfalls and billowing clouds. In this combination, the work is imbued with a devotional, almost sacred quality, borrowing visuals of the holy and filling it with the biological. The sticky, hairy, fleshy becomes something worth staring at with open fascination, the same way we are in awe of exotic plants, or deep-sea creatures, illuminating the sublime inherent in the corporeal.
Learn more by clicking ‚ÄúHappening Now‚ÄĚ in the Linktree above in our profile

events.title: ūüé® Death Circle
events.description:
ūĚĒáūĚĒĘūĚĒěūĚĒĪūĚĒ• ‚Ą≠ūĚĒ¶ūĚĒĮūĚĒ†ūĚĒ©ūĚĒĘ, ūĚĒĪūĚĒ¨ūĚĒ°ūĚĒěūĚĒ∂ ūĚĒ£ūĚĒĮūĚĒ¨ūĚĒ™ 5 ūĚĒĪūĚĒ¨ 7 ūĚĒďūĚĒź 

So many of us are carrying a lot right now. Personal losses, collective grief, the ache of what is happening in the world, and the grief we feel for our more-than-human kin and planet. We were never meant to carry any of this alone. What does it mean to live and die consciously? This circle is an offering to unburden ourselves in shared community and, in doing so, perhaps find more spaciousness and imaginative possibilities. 

Together we will co-create a sacred space to remember that these tender conversations about death, grief, cycles of change, and all of our connections in this web of existence once lived at the center of our communities. They are an integral part of our collective wellbeing. 

Death Circle is not a therapeutic setting or a grief support group, but an invitation to be present with one another as we explore these topics and reconnect more deeply with ourselves, each other, and the world around us. We welcome your curiosities, stories, questions, as well as your silence. There is no pressure to speak. All of it belongs ūüĖ§

Our hope is that this offering fosters clarity, nurtures care, and deepens our relational intimacy. 

Death Circle will be held on the last Sunday of every month, from 5-7pm. We‚Äôll have coffee and donuts, but feel free to bring your own snacks or any comfort items that support you.

We look forward to seeing you there ‚ÄĒ 
Joshua & Bri

events.title: ūüé® Advance Base & Moontype with likewindowpanes
events.description:
advance_base & moontypo are coming back to Greensboro with local support from likewindowpanes on Wednesday, February 25th ūüĎĺūüé∂ūüéĻ

Doors at 7pm and music at 8pm // $12 suggested 

This is going to be a really sweet show. We‚Äôre super thrilled to have these folks back in Greensboro. If you missed Advance Base for his winter tour in 2024 or Moontype last summer, this is your chance to see them both with Greensboro‚Äôs own, Panes. 

Advance Base is the melancholic soft rock recording project of Chicago, IL singer/songwriter Owen Ashworth (formerly of Casiotone for the Painfully Alone). Using a two-handed arsenal of electric piano, Omnichord, samplers, effect pedals & drum machines, Ashworth builds minimalist, heavy-hearted, nostalgia-obsessed ballads around his conversational baritone. The warm, electronic sound of Advance Base has been described as ‚Äúlo-fi,‚ÄĚ ‚Äúdepressed‚ÄĚ & ‚Äúweirdly uplifting.‚ÄĚ Advance Base released Horrible Occurrences in 2024.

Moontype is songwriter Margaret McCarthy‚Äôs dreamworld come to life. Based in Chicago, Illinois, the band features Emerson Hunton on drums, and Joe Suihkonen and Andrew Clinkman on guitar. Quiet emotions that McCarthy explores with her bass become, with the band, as large and as musically complex as a mood itself: cathartic, angsty, and unmet. Moontype released their debut album, Bodies of Water, in 2021 to critical acclaim.

panes is minimal introspective music with an engaging, dreamy listlessness. ever careening toward some kind of undiscovered self epiphany.

Flyer by: likewindowpanes

Bring your cold poems, the poems that keep your warm, poems about decay, poems about what comes after the thaw, poems of new starts, poems about the weather.

events.title: ūüé® Death Circle
events.description:
ūĚĒáūĚĒĘūĚĒěūĚĒĪūĚĒ• ‚Ą≠ūĚĒ¶ūĚĒĮūĚĒ†ūĚĒ©ūĚĒĘ, ūĚĒĪūĚĒ¨ūĚĒ°ūĚĒěūĚĒ∂ ūĚĒ£ūĚĒĮūĚĒ¨ūĚĒ™ 5 ūĚĒĪūĚĒ¨ 7 ūĚĒďūĚĒź 

So many of us are carrying a lot right now. Personal losses, collective grief, the ache of what is happening in the world, and the grief we feel for our more-than-human kin and planet. We were never meant to carry any of this alone. What does it mean to live and die consciously? This circle is an offering to unburden ourselves in shared community and, in doing so, perhaps find more spaciousness and imaginative possibilities. 

Together we will co-create a sacred space to remember that these tender conversations about death, grief, cycles of change, and all of our connections in this web of existence once lived at the center of our communities. They are an integral part of our collective wellbeing. 

Death Circle is not a therapeutic setting or a grief support group, but an invitation to be present with one another as we explore these topics and reconnect more deeply with ourselves, each other, and the world around us. We welcome your curiosities, stories, questions, as well as your silence. There is no pressure to speak. All of it belongs ūüĖ§

Our hope is that this offering fosters clarity, nurtures care, and deepens our relational intimacy. 

Death Circle will be held on the last Sunday of every month, from 5-7pm. We‚Äôll have coffee and donuts, but feel free to bring your own snacks or any comfort items that support you.

We look forward to seeing you there ‚ÄĒ 
Joshua & Bri

events.title: ūüé® DAGGERTEETH
events.description:
DAGGERTEETH TOMORROW TOMORROW TOMORROW.
.
ARE Y'ALL PUMPED CUZ WE CAN'T WAIT TO OPEN OUR DOORS TO YOU TOMORROW!
.
Teeth don't lie. They bite, they tear, they mark what they touch.

Traditional printmaking techniques meet zine culture's raw urgency. Punk aesthetics slash through the white cube aesthetic and screams to be heard.

Every piece here carries evidence of hands‚ÄĒink rolled, paper pressed, screens burned, images carved. The tactility of making things physical in a digital age. These works don't apologize for their rough edges. They weaponize them.

DAGGERTEETH celebrates artists who create outside institutional approval, distribution systems built on photocopiers and kitchen tables, aesthetics born from necessity and defiance. The underground made visible.

Sharp work for sharp teeth.

**February 21, 2026 | 5PM-8PM**

Irving Park Art & Frame

2800 Lawndale Dr. Ste 107, Greensboro, NC

.

.

.

events.title: ūüéĶ Sweet Megg
events.description:
Tonight, Thursday the 11th!
Doors at 7pm
Show at 8pm

Sweet Megg is a dynamic vocalist and songwriter originally from New York City, now based in Nashville. She began her career at just 16 as a songwriter and has worn many hats since: whitewater raft guide, DIY anti-folk artist, Parisian and New York jazz singer, honky-tonk angel, and circus ring leader.

Her diverse travels and experiences have imbued her music with rich history and boundless innovation. Her vocals are both expertly trained and refreshingly raw, making her sound relatable and captivating.

Megg‚Äôs music weaves together many threads of the American story‚ÄĒher Irish heritage, early blues training in high school, years immersed in the jazz world, explorations through the American South, inspiration from East Village poets, and the honky-tonk traditions of Nashville. The result is a sound that feels both timeless and refreshingly modern, offering a unique blend of old and new.

events.title: ūüéĶ Of Good Nature, Emanuel Wynter, and When We‚Äôre Sober
events.description:
Tomorrow, Friday the 12th!
Doors at 7:30pm
Show at 8:30pm

Of Good Nature is a high-energy live band from Charlotte, NC, blending rock, soul,
reggae, and world music into a powerful, feel-good experience. Known for their
vibrant musicianship and irresistible grooves, the band delivers dynamic
performances rooted in real instruments, funky rhythms, and crowd connection. No
clicks, no tracks, just pure chemistry.

Hailing from Charlotte, classically trained violinist and songwriter Emanuel Wynter is taking ears and stages by storm with a distinctive sound and a lively, high-spirited stage presence that is infectious to everyone in the room. Taking influence from artists such as Anthony Hamilton, Leon Bridges, and Gary Clark Jr., Emanuel‚Äôs sound is a unique, vibrant, and expressive blend of R&B and Rock that manages to be raw in emotion and refined, sonically. He finds a way to sing, not only through rich and soulful vocals, but through his electrifying violin playing that is, at times, quite reminiscent of electric guitar. With dynamic musicianship and sincere lyricism, Emanuel Wynter is sure to keep your toes tapping and your soul singing.

When We‚Äôre Sober is a genre-bending alt-rock band hailing from Greensboro, NC. Defined by a heavy grunge sound dripping with sonic energy, the band simultaneously explores diverse styles in their songwriting. Ranging from jangly pop-rock to heavy metal to folk-rock ballads, there‚Äôs few avenues the band won‚Äôt walk.

events.title: ūüéĶ Live Music: Tyler Millard, Tom Troyer, Allyn Raney, Drake Duffer
events.description:
Tonight, Tuesday the 9th!
Doors at 6pm
Show at 7pm

Tyler Millard is half of the husband and wife duo The Ghosts of Liberty. He discovered his love for creating music almost by accident, only picking up the guitar in 2006 as a hobby to pursue as he slowly lost his vision to a degenerative retinal disease. But what started as a hobby eventually became an obsession. Today, Tyler spends most of his days hacking his way through technology that was never truly designed for the blind in an effort to create music that is simultaneously substantive and catchy.

Tom Troyer is the ‚ÄúArtist in Resonance‚ÄĚ at Back Table Where he hosts the Monday Night Open Mic, curates the Silo Series, a genre blending and community building monthly First Friday gathering. And he coordinates the weekly sound bath ambient music for Ground and Groove yoga Wednesdays. His next single ‚ÄúLet Yourself Move On‚ÄĚ comes out April 30.

Allyn Raney is a performing musician and singer/songwriter from Winston-Salem, North Carolina. In a genre best described as soulful Americana, Allyn blends a variety of influences into a style that is completely her own. At 20 years old, she has performed on hundreds of stages, and is passionate about sharing the joy that music brings her personally with those who are open to listening.

Drake Duffer is a musician and songwriter from Winston-Salem, NC. A classical guitarist by trade, he grew up playing folk and bluegrass in the NC Piedmont, and enjoys weaving the two genres together. He is a founding member of the bands Early Gray and Mount Rosco.

events.title: ūüéĶ Trivia Night
events.description:
Last night was a blast! Thank you to everyone who came out!

We had our dude, Todd Turner spin the wheel to decide next Monday the 15th‚Äôs fate! It landed on on ‚ÄúWild Card‚ÄĚ which is basically spinners choice and Todd chose trivia! So join us next Monday for some trivia hosted by silentmicholdings and questions by generalclearinghouse 

silentmicholdings is going to start at 7pm and will continue to spin instrumentals through the trivia! It‚Äôs gonna be sick so come hang with us!

events.title: ūüéĶ Sam Morrow & Time Sawyer
events.description:
This Sunday the 14th!
Doors at 7pm
Show at 8pm

Sam Morrow has sound that exists outside of genre and geography. It‚Äôs his own version of modern-day American roots music: a mix of roadhouse rock & roll, bluesy R&B, and country-fried funky-tonk, driven forward by groove, grease, and guitars.

‚ÄúA sinewy, head-nodding listen‚Ä¶demands to be played loud at parties.‚ÄĚ -¬†Rolling Stone

‚ÄúThe record is buttressed by two strong singles: the keyboard-heavy opener ‚ÄúBy Your Side‚ÄĚ and the driving, twangy ‚ÄúMedicine Man,‚ÄĚ both perfectly showcasing Morrow‚Äôs whiskey-soaked, rich vocals, bringing to mind the ghost of Leon Russell, offering a powerful one-two Tyson-worthy punch opening the record.‚ÄĚ¬†- GLIDE

‚ÄúIt‚Äôs certainly the best rock album this year.‚ÄĚ -¬†Farce the Music

Time Sawyer‚Äôs name reflects the pull between the past and the future. The character Tom Sawyer evokes the rural background and love of home that the band shares. Time is a muse for songwriting; it‚Äôs the thread that runs through life, bringing new experiences and giving us a sense of urgency, while still connecting us with our past.

The folk-rock band has performed on the stages of some of the Southeast‚Äôs most iconic festivals, including Merlefest, Floydfest, Bristol Rhythm and Roots Reunion, Albino Skunk Music Festival, and Carolina in the Fall. They‚Äôve shared bills with American Aquarium, John Craigie, Hiss Golden Messenger, Langhorne Slim, John Moreland, Steep Canyon Rangers, The Wood Brothers, Susto, and many more.¬†¬†

events.title: ūüéĶ Mason P. Winfree, Jodi Jones & Jack Romano and The Romanaires
events.description:
Tonight, Sunday the 7th!
Doors at 6:30pm
Show at 7:30pm

Mason P. Winfree is an award-winning old-time musician and flatfoot dancer who has earned numerous ribbons, including first-place honors, at bluegrass and old-time fiddlers‚Äô conventions across North Carolina and Southwest Virginia, such as the Galax Old Fiddlers Convention and the Mount Airy Fiddlers Convention. Over the years, he has performed with several groups and has shared his craft by teaching and showcasing flatfoot dancing both nationally and abroad, including on PBS‚Äôs Song of the Mountains. *

Folksinger Jodi Jones is determined to boldly dream a better tomorrow in the wake of a broken and weary world. Deeply rooted in the musical traditions of her childhood home in the hills of Western Pennsylvania, her work is a poetic and unflinching account of the beauty and hardship of working class life in America. With defiant wit and innate musicality, she beautifies the bitter truths of our modern age into song as an act of love and solidarity. Art may not save us from our problems, but Jones believes it is crucial to surviving them with our humanity intact.
To sing is to live, to live is to hope, and to hope is to find the strength to fight another day in these dark and uncertain times. With guitar, banjo, fiddle, and even dulcimer in hand, she seeks to alchemize pain into purpose and break old ground to plant new seeds of hope and change wherever she goes.
Jack Romano & The Romanaires create a mythic sound of the 20th century as a backdrop for stories from modern American life.

Jack was born in the New Jersey highlands, a unique cultural¬†region whose ‚Äúhill people‚ÄĚ share as much in common with Appalachian communities in West Virginia as with cities like New York City and Philadelphia. He grew up listening to artists like Johnny Cash, John Denver, and Gene Autry, alongside Frank Sinatra,¬†Dion & the Belmonts, and The Spinners.

Megan Jean‚Äôs Secret Family is a fresh blend of the 70‚Äôs by way of the 90‚Äôs rock, soul, country, and jam, emphasizing vocal-driven danceable original songs that bring together generations of sound. In May of 2024, the band made their national radio debut on NPR‚Äôs Mountain Stage alongside notable acts like Keller Williams and Bettye Lavette. DC Music Review called them one of their ‚ÄúNew favorite acts‚ÄĚ following an energitc performance at 2024‚Äôs DelFest. The band was awarded a creativity grant from the Maryland State Arts Council to produce their debut full-length album PLOT (Play.Loud.On.Turntable) which will be released in Fall 2024. In the three years since their formation they have shared the stage with Fastball, The Spin Doctors, Cabinet, Joan Osbourne, and Cris Jacobs.

events.title: ūüéĶ Tom Hamilton Live
events.description:
Tomorrow, Friday the 5th!
Doors at 8pm
Show at 9pm

Tom Hamilton‚Äôs guiding creative principle is very simple: he doesn‚Äôt like to repeat himself artistically.For the Philadelphia-based singer/songwriter andguitarist, this mindset stems from a deep-seated motivation to keep pushing himself as an artist ‚ÄĒto delve into different lyrical themes and musical detours, to explore potentially uncomfortable and unfamiliar emotional places.‚ÄĚThat‚Äôs a thrilling prospect,‚ÄĚ says Hamilton. ‚ÄúTo find the deeper things inside and to open up those doors that I didn‚Äôt even know were there.‚ÄĚAs founding member of Brothers Past, American Babies, Joe Russo‚Äôs Almost Dead, Ghost Light and Billy and The Kids, Hamiltonhas written/recorded roughlya dozen albums and EPs and shared the stage with everyone from John Mayer to Bob Weir to Billy Strings.Hamilton‚Äôs debut record as a solo artist will be released in late 2025.

events.title: ūüéĶ MAMA Live
events.description:
Tonight, Sunday the 31st!
Doors at 7:30pm
Show at 8pm

McKinney is a bass player and singer-songwriter. She spent her formative years in southern West Virginia where she literally LIVED inside a music school. Deeply incapable of leaving the Appalachian mountains, but needing an escape from her 6,000 person hometown, she landed right around the bend in Asheville, NC.¬†

When McKinney isn‚Äôt on stage you might find her teaching music lessons at LEAF Global Arts, working shows at The Orange Peel, or at lock on the rugby pitch.¬†

McKinney‚Äôs music genre is about as fluid as the average Asheville resident‚Äôs sexuality. Her Indie Pop sound is blended with influence from her blues singer mother, a BFA in Jazz, and the 8 year old version of herself that just wants to play classic rock. Although pinning down her genre may be hard, you can consistently expect sad lyrics. You will probably enjoy her lyricism if you are emotionally suppressed and enjoy listening to sad songs to make yourself feel better.¬†¬†

MAMA is a Soul Shakin‚Äô Blues project from Asheville, NC. Fronted by Melissa McKinney, a vocalist known for her raw power, emotional delivery, and genuine connection with her audience, the band is quickly making a name for themselves in the blues world. MAMA earned Second Place at the International Blues Challenge this year in Memphis, TN. They also won the Merlefest Band Competition in 2025. Beyond their accolades, Melissa is a leader in the Asheville arts community. She serves as Programming Director for LEAF Global Arts, is the co-founder of the WTF (Women to the Front) Music Festival, and a board member of AVL Fest.

events.description:

Tommy Prine is a Nashville-bred songwriter carving out his own identity with unflinching honesty, dynamic guitar work, and a voice that bridges Americana, indie folk, and Southern storytelling. Following the breakout of his acclaimed debut album¬†This Far South¬†and the momentum around his new EP¬†Love Circle¬†(out now), Prine has emerged as one of the most compelling young artists in the scene ‚ÄĒ known for emotionally charged live shows, sharp songwriting, and a rapidly growing fanbase across the U.S. and Europe.

West Virginia songwriter and piano bard Brad Goodall established himself as a vivid storyteller with his 2022 debut Made In America, which boasted uproariously satirical character studies of sad, desperate men in the vein of Randy Newman and Warren Zevon. Where that album unfolded like a collection of biting short stories, his latest Hometown plays out like an autobiography..

events.title: ūü§Ě Animal Survival Science Fun with Dawn Cusick
events.description:
Get ready to dive into the wild world of animal survival! Through hands-on science fun with microscopes, real specimens, stories, and games, children will uncover the fascinating ways creatures protect, hunt, and thrive. Scientist and author, Dawn Cusick, will share from her adventurous books "Animals That Make Me Say Ouch!" and "Animals That Make Me Say Look Out!".

This event introduces amazing animals ‚ÄĒ from alligators and snakes to jellyfish, bats, and beetles through microscopes, games and specimens. Discover how each one uses its unique adaptations to stay one step ahead in nature‚Äôs game!

events.title: ūü§Ě Greensboro Public Library Anime Con: The Intense Audiovisuals of Hatsune Miku
events.description:
Greensboro Public Library Anime Con is delighted to announce our next featured panelist!

Dr. Clair Nguyen serves as Lecturer in Music Theory at UNC Greensboro. Her primary research focuses on the intersections of music theory and film & media studies that includes films, music videos, video games, theme park music, Japanese anime, and Hatsune Miku rhythm games. Her secondary research area centers on Vietnamese popular and traditional music, including the vŠĽćng cŠĽē tradition.  She currently serves as advisor for the UNCG Animation Club as well as co-chair for the Society of Music Theory‚Äôs Film and Multimedia interest group.

She will be presenting her panel "The Intense Audiovisuals of Hatsune Miku," an exploration of how synchronized sounds and onscreen action create thoughtful and powerful storytelling in Vocaloid and Hatsune Miku: Colorful Stage! music videos.

Come meet Clair and join in on the fun on Saturday, August 8 from 10 am to 5 pm!

Greensboro Public Library Anime Con 2026 is delighted to announce our first featured panelist!

Jesi Routh is a Japanese Language instructor at UNC Greensboro with a vast range of experience accumulated across many fields. Having started her Japanese language journey through the discovery of Japanese music, she has since found a deep love and respect for Japanese history, swordplay, and the writing system of Kanji.

Come meet Jesi and join in on the fun on Saturday, August 8 from 10 am to 5 pm!

events.title: ūüõćÔłŹ Next Market Event
events.description:
Merry Christmas & Happy Holidays ūüéĄ As we approach the end of the year , we are thankful for all of the love and support we‚Äôve gotten thus far. It‚Äôs never easy starting something new, you‚Äôve gotta just put your best foot forward and go. But with God, the right people around you and a community-centric town like Greensboro, there‚Äôs no way to lose ‚Ě§ÔłŹ so I appreciate everyone who has shared a post, told a friend, or dragged a family member to one of our events. We‚Äôve done a lot in 9 months thanks to y‚Äôall, looking forward to what the next year brings! 

And since it‚Äôs the giving season we figured we‚Äôd give you the date of our next event ūüėĄ February 1st! More details and vendor information coming soon . Stay Tuned, Stay Warm, Stay Safe! ūüę°

ūüďł | shottbyverde

events.title: ūü§Ě Special Meeting: Borders are prisons and prisons are borders
events.description:
Greensboro Revolutionary Socialists would like to invite you to join us this Monday, February 10 at 7pm at 1333 Grove St for a special meeting, featuring a talk by organizer Kristen Godfrey on their article, "Borders are prisons and prisons are borders" with a discussion to follow.

*

"The way working-class people experience borders all over the world is tied to the theft of land and surplus value. Settler-colonialism‚Äôs life source is criminality and illegality, which is why we must be anti-capitalist abolitionists, developing a critical analysis of how imperialists‚Äô tactics work to divide us."

*

Tell us how this new context impacts you. Let's think about how we can resist together.
 Group of revolutionary socialists in Greensboro, NC and members of Tempest Collective @tempest_mag

events.title: ūüéĶ Spotlight Launch Party feat. Latour b2b Jimmy D
events.description:
This Saturday we host the launch party of the new joint record from elsewhere‚Äôs two music directors Latour + Jimmy D, who‚Äôve teamed up to release ‚ÄúSpotlight‚ÄĚ on the iconic Central Station Records. 

Latour b2b Jimmy D for a 3.5hr set this Saturday
with support from Rav & Cobella. 

About the record:
Latour and Jimmy D are two music directors on the Gold Coast at elsewhere who‚Äôve come together to write Spotlight. (their second collab)

Jimmy‚Äôs history dates back to being a vinyl DJ on the Sydney circuit in the 90‚Äôs, we wanted to bring back the flavour of that era with new updated NRG, that holds weight on today‚Äôs systems and events. 

The record being a standout memorable tune of the night with its stellar vocal which sees the majority of the record stripped away leaving a sassy ear catching moment in the rave. Then slowly creeping back up to an all our NRG finale. Think donk basslines, 90s rave synths & fast grooving raw drums.

A record to make SURE the dancefloor is indeed dancing. And if you aren‚Äôt then, ‚Äúgetttt the f offfffff ‚Äú

events.title: ūü§Ě EICC Book Drive
events.description:
Throughout the month of April, Scuppernong will have a box at our register for this book drive for the youth program at Eastside Immigrant Community Center, a part of the Center for New North Carolinians at UNCG. cnnc.uncg ‚Ā†
‚Ā†
We're collecting grade school to YA books, with a comprehensive wishlist and guidelines at the EICC Book Drive link in our bio. Feel free to stop by the store to drop books in the box, or buy some on our website and leave us a note that it's for the drive! ‚Ā†
‚Ā†
ūüďöÔłŹūüíě‚Ā†
‚Ā†
#bookdrive #ScuppernongBooks #downtownGSO #greensboro

events.title: ūü§Ě TowneBank Summer Concert Series - The Shakedown
events.description:
Yesterday, we had the pleasure of celebrating the talented student artists behind this year‚Äôs Downtown Signal Box Student Art Project.

Surrounded by family, friends, and supporters, these young creatives were recognized for transforming ordinary signal boxes into vibrant works of public art that add color, creativity, and personality to downtown Greensboro.

Students received goodie bags and special maps highlighting the locations of signal boxes featuring student artwork, giving them the chance to explore downtown and see how their creativity is making a lasting impact on our community.

The celebration will continue this Thursday when artwork from several student participants is featured on the video board during the TowneBank Summer Concert Series at First National Bank Field as The Shakedown takes the stage.

To every student who shared their creativity with our community: thank you. Your artwork helps make downtown Greensboro a more vibrant, welcoming, and inspiring place for everyone. To the teachers who made this project possible, thank you for investing in your students and encouraging them to participate! 

Join us in congratulating these incredible young artists and enjoy a look back at yesterday‚Äôs reception through these photos.

events.title: ūüĆą Family-friendly songs with Mr. Sam of Scuppernong's Saturday Get-Downs
events.description:
Are you ready to celebrate Pride?!? Pride in our gender identity, gender expression, and sexual orientation happens year-round, and it's also great to have opportunities to celebrate together!

Join us at the Pyrle Theater in downtown Greensboro for a day of celebration on Saturday, June 20. The fun kicks off in the afternoon with family-friendly songs led by Mr. Sam of Scuppernong's Saturday Get-Downs, followed by a pizza dance party for youth and teens. To close out the night, the 21+ crowd will be treated to performances by drag superstars Thigh Fieri, Franny Starlight, and Brenda the Drag Queen, with DJ SmoothE providing tunes throughout the evening.

The Pyrle will have food and drinks available to keep the fun going. Tickets will be available soon through the Pyrle, so stay tuned!

events.title: ūüé® Public Tour: Mesoamerican Ballgame
events.description:
The 2026 fifaworldcup kicks off today with a match between M√©xico and South Africa - and in that spirit, we're offering a special public tour with curator √Āngel Gonzalez exploring the works on display in the Ancient American Gallery on June 18.

This free tour will focus on the Mesoamerican ballgame, which was played for more than 3,000 years. Discover how today's professional sports function in much the same way as this ancient game. This tour is being conducted in collaboration with consulmexraleigh.

Please register for your free ticket at the link in our bio.

events.title: ūüé® Recorrido especial con el curador √Āngel Gonz√°lez: El juego de pelota mesoamericano

Este recorrido gratuito se centrar√° en el juego de pelota mesoamericano, el cual se jug√≥ por m√°s de 3000 a√Īos. Descubre c√≥mo los deportes profesionales de hoy funcionan de forma similar a este juego antiguo. Este recorrido se realiza en colaboraci√≥n con consulmexraleigh.

events.title: ūüé® North Carolinians‚Äô Fight for Independence: The Soldier‚Äôs Experience
events.location: NCMA, Main Hall of East Building
events.description:
The statearchivesnc are bringing their "One-Day Wonder" pop-up exhibition to the NCMA this Friday, June 12, from 10 am-4 pm. In commemoration of America250NC, the State Archives created a traveling exhibit to explore the experiences of North Carolina soldiers fighting in the Revolutionary War.

Titled "North Carolinians‚Äô Fight for Independence: The Soldier‚Äôs Experience," this exhibit will provide the public with an opportunity to explore the topic through original records, rare reproductions, and informational exhibit panels.

As the name indicates, this is a one-day-only event. These historic pieces will be on view in the Main Hall of East Building.

You can learn more about America 250 at the State Archives at the link in our bio.

---------
Pictured: Pay Voucher for Edward Going, No Date
Treasurer‚Äôs and Comptroller‚Äôs Papers, Military Papers, Revolutionary Pay Vouchers, 1779-1782, nc_archives_photos

A pay voucher addressed to Edward Going for six pounds, twelve shillings, and four pence. The date is missing due to hole punches.

events.description:

events.title: ūüé® Screening: Always Looking: Titus Brooks Heagins
events.description:
Reminder: Join us on Friday evening, June 5, for a screening of the 2025 documentary, "Always Looking: Titus Brooks Heagins."

This film looks at the work of photographer Titus Brooks Heagins, whose photos document those marginalized by race, gender/sexual identity, and poverty. The film also explores his own challenges of capturing these stories while posing the question, "who can tell whose story?"

Get your tickets at the link in our bio.

events.title: ūüé® Knowing the West
events.description:
With over 25 museums and 65 art galleries in the Triangle area, and plenty more across the state, INDY put together a small local list of places to see art this summer. 

The NCMA's latest exhibition, Knowing the West, made the list! This show will challenge your preconceived notions of the American West and offer new perspectives on the art and culture of this time and place in our nation's history.

See INDY's full list at the link in our bio.

--------
Pictured: Albert Bierstadt, Sierra Nevada Morning, 1870, Oil on canvas, 55 1/4 x 85 1/2 in., Gilcrease Museum

events.title: ūüé® Still. Moving: Free Dance Performance by Frankie Lee III
events.description:
The next "Still. Moving" free dance performance at the NCMA will be this Saturday, June 6, at 11 am and 1 pm in the Museum Park. Join us as Frankie Lee III (fleedance) presents a new work inspired by Wim Botha's sculptures, "Prism 5" and "Prism 7."

Botha‚Äôs sculptures explore weighty questions of history, status, power, and religion. Suspended wings hover above the ground, lifted‚ÄĒand restrained‚ÄĒby thin rectangular supports that seem to both elevate and tether them.

Are these the wings of unseen, silent angels? Or do they belong to something darker? Through movement, Lee and the dancers enter that tension between ascension and gravity, freedom and control.

--------
Images 1 and 3: Wim Botha, Prism 5 (2013) and Prism 7 (2014), bronze and stainless steel, dimensions variable, Purchased with funds from the Modern and Contemporary Deaccession Fund
Image 2: Frankie Lee III, courtesy of the artist

events.title: ūüé® Official Public Opening of 'Please Be Seated - North Carolina' with Dance Activation
events.description:
The fencing around the Museum Park's latest installation, "Please Be Seated - North Carolina," created by PaulCocksedge, is coming down! We'll be celebrating the official public opening tomorrow, Tuesday, June 2, with a special dance activation.

Directed by choreographers Christa Oliver and Betsy Graves, the performance features live musicians alongside over 40 dancers from ThePanoramicDanceProject (NCSU), Broughton Dance (CapsDance), and communities across North Carolina.

The performance begins at 6:30 pm and is free to attend. The dance should last about 15 minutes, so make sure to get there on time!

Click the link in our bio for more details.

events.description:
Exciting news! The NCMA's latest outdoor installation, "Please Be Seated - North Carolina," created by PaulCocksedge, will open to the public in just a few days - on Tuesday, June 2.

To celebrate, we are hosting a special dance performance at 6:30 pm sharp featuring live musicians and dozens of dancers from ThePanoramicDanceProject, Broughton Dance (CapsDance), and communities across the state.

Join us for a vibrant celebration of movement, music, and public art!

Click the link in our bio for all the details.

events.title: ūüé® Hildaland
events.description:
This weekend! Don‚Äôt miss two incredible shows at The ArtsCenter. 

Firstly, on Thursday night don‚Äôt miss the chance to see New England duo Hildaland as they continue their tour through the southeast! 

A contemporary collage of Scottish and American traditional music, Hildaland is a duo that approaches geographic borders as markers of exploration rather than boundaries to be wary of. Fiddle player Louise Bichan, originally from Orkney, Scotland, and mandolinist Ethan Setiawan, originally from Goshen, Indiana, have taken their duo from traditional Scottish roots through to progressive bluegrass and jazz, with music inspired largely by places and the journeys between them, both physical and musical. 

Then on Saturday, Pop Up Chorus returns with a special Broadway edition centering the songs from the smash hit musical Hamilton, which is celebrating its 11th year with a national tour now playing at DPAC! Come sing songs from the show with PopUp Broadway here at The ArtsCenter on June 6!

Tickets on sale at artscenterlive.org

events.title: ūüé® Horse Lords
events.description:
JUST ANNOUNCED: SUNDAY 10/11 8:00 PM ‚ÄĒ Horse Lords (local support TBD) 

Horse Lords were founded in Baltimore, Maryland in 2010; they evolved from the experimental collective Teeth Mountain and began as a trio with drummer Sam Haberman, guitarist Owen Gardner, and bassist Max Eilbacher, soon adding alto saxophonist Andrew Bernstein to the core ensemble. Though Horse Lords grew out of the fertile Baltimore noise and leftfield rock scene, a storied environment for artists and weirdos that has nurtured many an influential outsider band (Lungfish, Matmos), their approach is more omnivorous than the stippled rhythms of instrumental electric rock would indicate. 

Across sixteen years of frequent touring, Horse Lords have released ten studio, live, and collaboration albums in addition to a split EP with fellow Baltimoreans Lower Dens, a handful of singles and compilation tracks. For RVNG‚Äôs FRKWYS series, the band collaborated with Berlin-based composer Arnold Dreyblatt, a onetime student of La Monte Young and Pauline Oliveros whose just intonation chamber music was a crucial part of the post-Fluxus Downtown New York City environment. 

With the relocation of three of the four members to Germany beginning in 2021, the potential for more further creative exploration, and performance opportunities including the Moers Festival (with an 11-piece lineup, interpreting Julius Eastman‚Äôs music as well as their own), the interdisciplinary phases of the band‚Äôs music was poised for fascinating new directions. The band observes ‚ÄúWe are always trying to balance working within the limitations of the quartet and pushing beyond them.

For their second studio album on RVNG Intl., Demand To Be Taken To Heaven Alive!, Horse Lords have followed this notion and augmented the instrumentation, expanding the format slightly to include bass clarinetist Madison Greenstone (TAK Ensemble), trombonist Weston Olencki (Nate Wooley, RAGE Thormbones) and vocalists Nina Guo (Departure Duo) and Evelyn Saylor (Holly Herndon, Caterina Barbieri) across a program of twelve original compositions. 

Read more/buy tickets at https://artscenterlive.org/performances-events

events.title: ūüéĶ AJ Lee & Blue Summit
events.description:
Very pleased to welcome ajleebluesummit back to The Ramkat on October 22, 2026, with special guests thecodysisters! Doors at 7 PM, show at 8 PM.

Tickets go on sale at 10 AM Eastern this Friday, June 12, at TheRamkat.com! 

AJ Lee & Blue Summit are an award-winning, energetic, charming, and technically jaw-dropping band quickly rising on the national roots music scene. Originally based in Santa Cruz, California, the group met as teenagers, picking and jamming together as kids at local bluegrass festivals until one day, they decided they would be a band. After almost a decade on the road, they have established themselves as one of the most exciting and dynamic groups in Bluegrass.

The band is AJ Lee on mandolin, fiddler Jan Purat, Sullivan Tuttle on guitar, and Sean Newman on Bass. The band carries that youthful, festival-parking-lot energy with them still today, but at the same time there‚Äôs a genuine ease and confidence to their music making. Their critically acclaimed third studio album City Of Glass was released on Signature Sounds records in 2024. 2025 saw the release of a series of covers featuring guests such as Lindsay Lou, Trey Hensley, Ahn Phung and Kyle Tuttle called Cover to Cover V.1.#ajleeandbluesummit #thecodysisters #theramkat #wsnc #supportlivemusic

events.description:
Happy to announce An Evening with thegalentines.band in  Gas Hill Drinking Room on July 8, 2026! Doors at 7 PM, show at 8 PM.

Tickets are on sale NOW at TheRamkat.com! 

The Galentines are an all-girl indie-rock band who are the very heart of the local music scene in Boulder, Colorado. They are known for their stunning harmonies blended into epic instrumental solos that remind the listener of classic rock bands such as Fleetwood Mac and Pink Floyd, while maintaining their punk edge with biting lyricism. Their music ‚Äúcelebrates the complexity of womanhood, capturing all of its challenges and triumphs‚ÄĚ (Medium), while breaking the barriers that define the female experience. Encompassing multiple genres, including indie, punk and rock, the girls just released their heavily anticipated single Eden, which is now available on all streaming platforms. #thegalentines #gashilldrinkingroom #wsnc #supportlivemusic

events.title: ūüéĶ Nether Hour
events.description:
Happy to welcome netherhour  in from Austin, Texas to Gas Hill Drinking Room on June 24, 2026! Doors at 7 PM, show at 8 PM.

Tickets are on sale NOW at TheRamkat.com!

‚ÄúWe don‚Äôt play country music, but we‚Äôre good ole boys from the country.‚ÄĚ

Down in the heart of Austin, TX, where the music runs thicker than the heat, there‚Äôs a band that‚Äôs been making some serious noise. They call themselves Nether Hour, and they‚Äôve been turning every dive bar and backroad juke joint into a full-blown musical revival. The band is a trio of unapologetic rebels, each one a powerhouse of sonic defiance. Bobby Flaco delivers gritty vocals and razor-sharp guitar riffs, while Bishop unleashes searing lead guitar and mandolin work, with Bercy laying down bass lines that slam right into your gut. Nether Hour is a relentless sonic melting pot, fusing the swampy grooves of Louisiana with Texas-sized doses of funk, blues, soul, and rock ‚Äėn‚Äô roll. #netherhour #gashilldrinkingroom #wsnc #supportlivemusic

events.title: ūüéĶ Al Olender with Fruit Bats at The Ramkat
events.description:
Excited  to welcome alolender to The Ramkat on July 16, 2026 in support of Fruit Bats! 

Get your tickets NOW at TheRamkat.com!

Songwriter Al Olender tries to grasp the brief, beautiful moments that make up our lives in slow-motion but finds that the tighter she holds on, the more these frames fall into a soupy blur. Change is our only constant, but what if we wanted to steep in the now? What if we could stay right here? Olender captures the anxieties of an ever-moving world with a sweet and silly sincerity on her second full-length album The Worrier, never shying away from staring the truth right in the face. Exploring anxiety, sexuality and anarchy, both in herself and in her relationships, Olender‚Äôs acoustic, bluesy melodies point to the butterflies of first crushes, the gut-punching realities of responsibility and the eternal romance of friendship. #alolender #fruitbats #theramkat #wsnc #supportlivemusic

events.title: ūüé® ADWS Annual Meeting
events.description:
ART CRUSH is evolving, and we‚Äôre doing it with intention.

This year, we‚Äôre focusing on fewer, more thoughtfully planned events to create stronger experiences for artists, attendees, and the businesses that make the Arts District of Winston-Salem thrive.

Here‚Äôs what 2026 looks like:
ūüĆą May ‚Äď Color the District
ūüŹôÔłŹJuly ‚Äď Winston-Salem Weekend
ūüéČ September ‚Äď Hispanic Heritage Month
ūüõćÔłŹ November ‚Äď Arts District Celebration

Each event is designed to feel meaningful, vibrant, and well-supported, with clearer themes, deeper engagement, and more space to do things really well.

What‚Äôs new for artists:
We‚Äôre introducing a $25 participation fee with 35 paid artist spots per event, plus 5 scholarship-funded spots for emerging artists. This helps sustain ART CRUSH while keeping access and opportunity at the center of what we do.

One more shift:
We‚Äôre moving away from annual memberships. Artists can now participate event by event: simple, flexible, and clear. (Full details coming to our website soon!)

Want the full story?
Join us for the ADWS Annual Meeting at chadschai 
ūüďÖ February 27 | ‚Źį 6:00‚Äď7:30 PM

Come curious. Bring a friend who‚Äôs new to ART CRUSH. Stay for the conversation.‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč

events.title: ūüé® This Side of Crazy
events.location: The Beall Theatre at Theatre Alliance - 651 Pilot View St NW Winston Salem, NC 27101
events.description:
Don‚Äôt be an outsider looking in! Enjoy a matinee with us this Saturday!

‚ÄúThis Side of Crazy,‚ÄĚ by Del Shores, is next in Theatre Alliance‚Äôs Beall Theatre! When three feuding sisters are summoned home by their popular gospel-music composing mother to perform one final musical concert, old tensions flare, and the outcome is anything but blessed and holy! Grab a front-row seat to the latest by the southern writer who brought us ‚ÄúSordid Lives,‚ÄĚ ‚ÄúSouthern Baptist Sissies,‚ÄĚ and ‚ÄúThe Trials and Tribulations of a Trailer Trash Housewife!‚ÄĚ

September 20 and 27 at 12 PM and 3 PM

Tickets: $15

Get them here: https://theatrealliance.ws/box-office/

Rated R for language, sexual content, mature subject matter

LOCATION: The Beall Theatre at Theatre Alliance - 651 Pilot View St NW Winston Salem, NC 27101

Signature Drink: The Sandi Patti

Running Time: 2 hours, with one 15-minute Intermission

(same physical building as Ihrie Theatre, but the entrance is on Pilot Street- free parking in the upper lot adjacent to the building. (The Centenary lot beside our building.))

Pictured: Katy Carroll

Photos: Neil Jester - Fine Art Photography

ūüé∂ With a sweeping score by legendary composer Stephen Schwartz‚ÄĒthe genius behind Wicked, Pippin, and Godspell‚ÄĒThe Prince of Egypt transforms the story of Moses into an unforgettable theatrical experience.

From the palaces of Pharaohs to the parting of the Red Sea, this epic musical is powered by music that moves both heart and soul.

ūüé≠ The Prince of Egypt storms the stage this summer at Theatre Alliance!

events.title: ūüé® The Shark is Broken
events.location: Beall Theatre at Theatre Alliance, 651 Pilot View St, Winston-Salem, NC
events.description:
Break a Leg to our Cast and Crew of ‚ÄúThe Shark is Broken,‚ÄĚ opening this afternoon in the Beall Theatre at 3 PM and also playing Sunday at 5:30 PM! (Missing from photo are Jeremy Engel, Alex Fredwall, and John Wilson).

The first summer blockbuster movie is being filmed‚ÄĒbut no one working on the film would know it. Dive deep into the tumultuous, murky waters of the making of a major motion picture with testy, feuding costars, unpredictable weather, and a shark prop whose constant breakdowns are looking like an omen for the future of the movie. In this comedy co-written by Ian Shaw and Joseph Nixon, the short tempers of Jaws stars Robert Shaw (father of co-writer Ian), Richard Dreyfuss, and Roy Scheider take center stage as they bond, argue, drink, gamble, and pray for an end to the shoot, not knowing it will change their lives forever

Make a double-feature of It and catch ‚ÄúThe Prince of Egypt,‚ÄĚ also playing this weekend!

The Beall Theatre at Theatre Alliance (upper level, upper parking lot), 651 Pilot View St, Winston-Salem, NC

Saturday, Aug 16 at 3 PM
Sunday, Aug 17 at 5:30 PM
Saturday, Aug 23 at 3 PM and 5:30 PM

Photos: Neil Jester - Fine Art Photography

Tickets: https://theatrealliance.ws/box-office/dl

events.title: ūüĆą K for Kids Alphabet Movie Night
events.location: lGBTQ Center of Durham Warehouse on Hunt
events.description:
ABCDEFGHIJ ‚ÄúK‚ÄĚ for Kids this Alphabet Movie Night we will be gathering at the lGBTQ Center of Durham Warehouse on Hunt for a Kid & Family Film. We will be watching the Marvel animated feature ‚ÄúInto the Spiderverse!‚ÄĚ This Saturday the 30th of May at 6:30pm we will all be friendly neighborhood SpiderFans!

We will have seats and popcorn a plenty, but please bring a blanket or pillow if you are a floor person. Also Pajamas and Stuffies always welcome, so you can make the transition from the movie to the bed that much easier!

Hope to see yall soon!

*Note: this movie , and our subsequent feature, were selected by audience members whom attended the last feature! So please join us and help us select the films you'd like to see.

#family #lgbtq #queer #kidsmovie #familyfun #community #pajamaparty #popcorn

events.title: ūüĆą Get Shit Done
events.description:
Some of the biggest barriers to survivor safety are finance related. While we do our best to put cash directly into survivors hands, in tandem, we thought it would be awesome to create a body doubling/coworking space where we can help each other executive function. As well as offer relevant financial resources! That‚Äôs why we are launching ‚ÄúGet Shit Done‚ÄĚ evenings. They will occur biweekly and be available in person or online (email advocate@lgbtqcenterofdurham.org for the virtual link). If you or someone you know is a money expert who wants to share their knowledge with queer/trans survivors, please reach out!

Image Descriptions/Alt Text:
Slide 1: Background is a wood grain table but in a lavender and dark purple hue. On top of the table, acting as the base of the image, is an open spiral notebook. The metal spirals are gold and the paper is white and lined. There is a faded coffee mug ring on the paper. In the top right corner of the image is a crumbled up piece of paper. On the right side of the image is a black pen. At the bottom right corner of the image is a folded set of marble colored glasses. On the spiral notebook in a black pen-like handwriting font that reads ‚Äú‚ÄúGet Shit Done, Every Other Tuesday‚ÄĚ. Below that reads ‚Äú(bolded) 5:30-7p, 114 Hunt St‚ÄĚ all highlighted in purple. Below that it reads, ‚ÄúIn person & Virtual Options‚Äú (underlined)‚ÄĚ With purple and pink stars on the right of it. Below that reads ‚ÄúA virtual space to do the $$ things we‚Äôve all been avoiding. Come work on taxes, budgeting, filling out that form... whatever you need!‚ÄĚ At the bottom of the notebook page is the SQSN mushroom logo and Southern Queer Survivor Network in all purple bold+caps font.

 #survivors #lgbtq #durhamnc #economicjustice #queer #trans

events.title: ūüĆą Welcome in Wednesday: Show and Tell Talent Show
events.location: LGBTQ Center of Durham, 114 Hunt St
events.description:
Put Some Play in your Day!

Welcome in Wednesday with Stormie Daie, meetups for youth 5-12, and families held at the LGBTQ Center of Durham at 114 Hunt St and participating community spaces.  This is your chance to engage the children in the LGBTQ Durham community by sharing space and activities together. Welcome in Wednesday will prioritize fun through social gathering and activities emphasizing nature, science, sharing, education, reading, and hands on crafts.

This time will serve to create connections amongst youth and families and strengthen community ties with and amongst younger lgbtq folks and their families.

As well we will inform the children and families with the happenings of the LGBTQ Center through partnerships with likeminded groups and community partners.

For questions, concerns, registration or interest in sponsoring/supporting Welcome in Wednesday, contact Stormie Daie at sdaie@lgbtqcenterofdurham.org

4/22 -  Terrarium
4/29 - Tea Party
5/6 - Kiteing

5/20 - Dress Up Tea Party
5/22 - Show and Tell Talent Show

*activities are subject to change due to weather and space/supply needs

This is not child care, parents or guardians or care takers are expected to be present.

events.title: ūüĆą Alphabet Movie Nite: Space Jam
events.description:
Let's get reeling and rolling for our first ‚ÄúAlphabet Movie Nite‚ÄĚ here at the Queerhouse Theater. This first screening is ‚ÄúK‚ÄĚ for the Kids with all ages welcome! 

We will be watching the 90s multimedia sports comedy animated classic ‚ÄúSpace Jam,‚ÄĚ starring NC‚Äôs very own, plus all your favorite cartoon athletes - and hosted by Durham's own, Stormie Daie! 

Screening is at May 9 at 6:30pm with a runtime of 1:30min. Space is ample but seating is limited so please bring a blanket or lawn chair if possible. 

Also feel free to bring your best stuffie and wear your best pjs so you can crawl home and get in bed promptly after the finish of the film! 

Popcorn provided. Feel free to bring outside meals/snacks for you and yours.

Come early - parking is limited, primarily street parking.

events.title: ūü§Ě Ska√įi - Ambient Underground Beats
events.description:
It‚Äôs Pride Month, and Arcana has a confession to make- we‚Äôre crazy in love with our neighbors at Rubies.‚ú®ūüŹ≥ÔłŹ‚ÄćūüĆąūüŹ≥ÔłŹ‚Äć‚ößÔłŹ

Love between bars is wildly underreported and underappreciated it, so we‚Äôre making it public! ūüíč

This Saturday, when we are basking in the glow of some delicious live jazz from Trio Factual, we still might be secretly (not so secretly) wondering what Fifi Hifi is up to at Adulting.. Or maybe we‚Äôre thinking about the song we might sing to impress our beloved Sky at karaoke on Sunday. ūü•ā

It‚Äôs confusing to us sometimes to be a lesbian bar, and pine SO so hard for Bruce. ūü•Ļ We can‚Äôt tell you how many times we‚Äôve locked up our doors at midnight and gone racing up the stairs to Rubies for a nightcap, an hour of dancing, and maybe some cute bathroom selfies. 

We love the staff and the vibe and the programming- y‚Äôall are just the best neighbors a gay could ever have. The tamales are always hot (but the bartenders are even hotter).

This Friday, for instance! Please please, come out and support Ska√įi for some ambient underground beats at Arcana on Friday from 9-11pm, THEN spiral up the stairs to Rubies for their Pride party with Sad Muse Club. And mark your calendars, because Sad Muse Club will be back at Arcana on July 16th! If you like Adulting at Rubies, check out the vibe at Arcana on June 20th with Get Out of My Dreams, featuring the one and only Fifi Hifi and Vespertine. 

Can‚Äôt wait to see you here, or there, or here AND there ūüĆąūü™©

xoxo Arcana ūüźČ ūüėė

events.title: ūü§Ě Bachata and Dance Social
events.description:
the first week of juneūüĆą 

we have a cuuuute week lined up for you!

First we have our monthly bachata and dance socialūüĆě free class!! but grab a drink and tip your instructors before taking your new moves to the dance floorūü™©

Wednesday is the monthly triangleareapaganalliance meet & greet! come mingle with other local pagans! 

Thursday we have lowolfmusic ūüé∂ they‚Äôre a queer folk country artist from Asheville with a unique and rebellious energy ‚ú®

Friday we have the sexiest, sultriest Marmalade everūüíč come see some exciting sexy performances with a dj set by dizquefresa in betweenūüé∂‚Ě§ÔłŹ‚ÄćūüĒ•

on Saturday we have some nice ambient vibes with Queen Plz! come hang with your friends and enjoy a nice, cute night out! 

then Sunday we have some good live music with Brianna McGeehan and Ashley Virginia!!! ūü™©‚ú®ūüé∂ūüíč

GAG! is launching a dance party on January 9th! TRAMP was created in response to the community for a dance party ‚ÄĒ a raw, no disruptions, nonstop music dance party where you can dance freely. Once Bitter Social opened, a new downtown bar by the makers of Bitter Sweet and Johnson Street Yacht Club, it became clear this space was made for just that!

This small intimate space creates a different vibe from any GAG party that promotes getting closer with each other; whether it‚Äôs dancing or conversing in one their dark corners.
GAG is bringing two special guest DJs, featuring, Pisces Prince (Gaven Simon) from NYC ‚ÄĒ featured in So.Gay in 2025 and GHO3 SPINZ from GAG to Death this past Halloween, will pump up the BPMs FOR EVERYONE tramping on the dance floor!

events.title: ūüĆą HIV/STI Testing w/ Alliance @ Center
events.description:
This Week @ The Center!

Sunday, 06/07: No Events or Programs

Monday, 06/08: HIV/STI Testing w/ Alliance @ Center, 5-7 PM + 
Transgender Discussion Group @ Center, 7-8 PM 

Tuesday, 06/09: No Events or Programs

Wednesday, 06/10: Youth Coffee House - Raleigh @ 
Blackbird Books and Coffee 5-7 PM 

Thursday, 06/11: Older Adults Coffee Gathering @ Center, 9:30 AM + 
Queer Professional Happy Hour @ Bittersocial 6-8 PM 

Friday, 06/12: Game Night! @ Center, 6 - 9 PM + 
LGBTQ+ Game Night @ Sidequest 6PM + Heated Rivalry Dance Party 
@ Pourhouse 9 PM - 12 AM

Saturday, 06/13: Homebody Yoga @ Slow Power Yoga 12-1 PM + Pride Market @ The Hartwell 12-4 PM + Mrs. Romper Romp BDay Bash @ Le Dive 1 PM - Until +
Alphabet Soup Market @ Downtown Cary Park 5-10 PM + Triangle Queer Artist Collective @ The Center, 6 - 8 PM 

Sunday, 6/14:  Pride Service @ UUFR 10:30 AM + Queens Supporting Queens @ Le Dive 12 - 2 PM + Pop Up For Pride Market @ Gateway Plaza 12-3 PM + Pride Fest  @ Pickleback 12:30 - 8 PM 

Keep an eye on our calendar for drop-in times and program changes!

Interested in volunteering at this years Out! Raleigh Pride? Applications are now open and can be found using the link here: https://www.lgbtcenterofraleigh.com/volunteer-orp

events.title: ūüĆą Elton John Impact Awards Podcast
events.location: iHeartRadio or wherever you get your podcasts
events.description:
As a beneficiary organization of the Elton John Impact Awards, The LGBT Center of Raleigh is able to continue driving meaningful change for LGBTQ+ communities by expanding program offerings and providing much-needed community supplies. ūüĆą. 

This program goes beyond celebration, providing critical funding and visibility to organizations like ours, helping expand life-affirming programs and reach more people who need support.

We‚Äôre also excited to celebrate the trailblazers being recognized for shaping culture through their sustained impact on the LGBTQ+ community.

Don‚Äôt forget to tune in starting June 1 on iHeartRadio or wherever you get your podcasts to hear powerful stories from this year‚Äôs incredible honorees, as well as our Executive Director, Kori Hennessey, on how this grant support helps drive our work and expands our impact.

#EltonJohnImpactAwards #LGBTQ #Equality

events.title: ūüĆą Camp ASPYRE 2026 Registration Opens
events.description:
Registration for Camp ASPYRE 2026 opens in exactly ONE WEEK on June 1st!! Heart + share this if you're as excited as we are!

ASPYRE Youth Leadership Camp is a powerful experience designed to empower young members of the LGBTQ community and their allies to achieve personal growth through advocacy, activism, and leadership development. Campers will build confidence, learn how to organize and advocate for their communities, and connect with other young people who are passionate about creating positive change.

ASPYRE Youth Leadership Camp welcomes youth ages 13 to 18 who are still in high school and are ready to step into leadership and make a meaningful difference in their communities.

Have questions? Reach out to the ASPYRE team at aspyre@lgbtcenterofraleigh.com for more information.

#CampASPYRE #ASPYRE2026

events.title: ūü§Ě The Wiley Fosters - Last Show at Mystery Brewing Company
events.description:
Today, we‚Äôre sharing sad news. After almost seven years, Mystery Brewing Company will be closing its doors at the end of the day on October 31st.
.
.
This has been an incredibly difficult decision. We‚Äôve always been undercapitalized, and it‚Äôs been a struggle to operate for some time now. We‚Äôve suffered a string of pretty bad luck over the past couple of years: equipment failures, construction and permitting delays, storm-related outages and losses. The end result is that we can no longer afford to operate.
.
.
Over the course of the next week, we‚Äôll be doing the best we can to celebrate everything we love about Mystery to give it a fond farewell, releasing some favorite old beers that we‚Äôve had kicking around in the back of the cooler, and trying to say goodbye the best way we can. If you get a chance, please come and visit us, say hi, and enjoy a pint, a meal, or a night of music for the last time. The Wiley Fosters ‚Äď our amazing house band ‚Äď will be playing our last show at the pub on the evening of October 27th.
.
.
We‚Äôre enormously proud of the community that has grown up around Mystery, and we‚Äôll miss being here every day for Hillsborough.
.
.
With one last raised glass,
.
.
Erik Lars Myers
Founder, Brewer, CEO
Mystery Brewing Company

events.title: ūü§Ě The Sunday Show (free) stand up comedy
events.description: Today‚Äôs #BeerChurch is dogfishhead‚Äôs Palo Santo Marron (2015). An unfiltered, unfettered, unprecedented brown ale aged in handmade wooden brewing vessels. The caramel and vanilla complexity unique to this beer comes from the exotic Paraguayan Palo Santo wood from which these tanks were crafted. Palo Santo means "holy tree," and its wood has been used in South American wine-making communities. This highly roasty and malty brown ale clocks in at 12% ABV. Pub is open from 11:30 AM - 10 PM, and The Sunday Show (free) stand up comedy starts at 8 PM! #hillsboroughNC

events.title: ūü§Ě Live music from writeplayrepeat
events.description: It‚Äôs a soup & sandwich kind of day. Head to the Pub for our Short Rib & Pumpkin Cassoulet with white beans, tomatoes, herbs, and leeks. We also have five sandwiches on the October menu including the Edamame Pate with local sprouts, cucumbers, roasted peppers, arugula, and herb aioli on sourdough. We had to postpone the savinggracenc adoption event due to weather, but the Pub is still open all day! Live music from writeplayrepeat tonight at 8:30 PM! See you in #HillsboroughNC.

events.title: ūüéĶ Collage Club
events.description:
THIS WEDNESDAY, MAY 20TH:

Join us for Collage Club in the Theater with joseph_rafferty 

Collage Club is a monthly community gathering at The Fruit. Rooted in Surrealism, we use provided materials - substrates (base), scissors, glue sticks, found objects, pre-cut images‚ÄĒto explore visual play, personification, and the subconscious. 

Each session begins with a theme and prompts designed to encourage "creating first and thinking later." All experience levels are welcome to join this creative laboratory where things that look like things become something else entirely!

Collage Club is a creative session lead by our friend and renowned creative mystic, Joseph Rafferty.

All needed materials are included with the session entry fee.

events.title: ūüéĶ MJ: The Essentials
events.description:
THIS WEEKEND: rdhproductions presents
MJ: The Essentials ‚ÄĒ a live dance tribute celebrating the legacy and artistry of Michael Jackson through a chronological showcase of music, dance, and visual storytelling.

Under the creative direction of Romell D. Harris, this is not just a tribute ‚ÄĒ it‚Äôs a reimagined performance experience. Expect signature choreography, powerful storytelling, and a fresh spin on the MJ catalog. From the anthems you love to the most electrifying performance moments, this showcase will honor the legacy while pushing artistic boundaries.

This live performance experience features:
‚ÄĘ Timeline-based show (Jackson 5 era ‚Üí Thriller era ‚Üí 90s stadium era ‚Üí Legacy finale)
‚ÄĘ Theatrical transitions
‚ÄĘ Visual projections
‚ÄĘ Live monologues 
‚ÄĘ Originally reimagined choreography

It's here for a two-day matinee run with doors opening at 3:30pm both this afternoon and Sunday the 17th.

THEN, Remember the time you fell in love with rdhproductions special 2-night weekend run of MJ: The Essentials, a Michael Jackson live dance tribute. 

NEXT WEDNESDAY, come out for this month's iteration of Collage Club hosted by our favorite joseph_rafferty 

THEN, NEXT SATURDAY fasterfaster.drm takes us through the weekend with HARDGROOVE feat. wormhole-inducing sets by prodbyeuuan emelcie_music and mofle.919

events.title: ūüéĶ Go Figure Figure Drawing Session
events.description:
NEXT MONDAY 5/4:

Always wanted to try out Figure Drawing? or perhaps you're looking to get back into the practice after some time off? Or.... you don't care too much for drawing but just want to meet local creatives in our natural habit. If any of this resonates with you, you're in luck. 

Go Figure is a drawing group founded to bring community together and make figure drawing accessible. This isn't a class but a chance to try something new out in a safe, supportive environment.

You are welcome to bring whatever drawing supplies you are currently finding inspiration in & ideally a mobile easel if you have one, but please, nothing that makes a mess... 
*ahem* NO dripping, squirting, or splashes. ūüé®ūüĎ®‚Äćūüé® 

Doors open at 6:30. Drawing is from 7pm-9pm.
Mature audiences only. 18+ only.

*Absolutely NO photography of the model or the space while the event is active.*

events.title: ūüéĶ Kink Practice Open House
events.description:
Hello HELLOOOO, what a delightful little last minute surprise we have in store for you HUMP heads here!

The lovely folks behind Kink Practice serve us a delicious Open House treat next Saturday.

Watch demonstrations by rope, fire, and other adult practitioners and learn how to get involved in the local community!

Meet and chat with locals who are excited to show off their skills (no audience participation permitted) and keep you entertained before the film festival begins. Check out the info booth to get linked up to classes and educational opportunities in the area.

Featured presenters:
poly_anthus
ties_by_oliver
hounds.tooth1

This short pop-up is just a *quick teaser* before the pleaser aimed to titillate your senses and get you in the zone. Discipline is a virtue, SO DON'T BE LATE! 

Doors to the Theater will open at 6:40pm-ish, with demos beginning promptly at 7pm and will end around 7:40pm‚ÄĒ just in time for you to get settled in if you stay for the festival!

This event is FREE, 21+ ONLY, and DOES NOT include automatic admission to HUMP Film Festival.

{{Festival ticket holders will use a separate entrance (main Warehouse doors) for access to the Theater prior to the show.}}

See you there!

events.title: ūüĆą Taking Up Space with Sarah LaTour
events.description:
Iiiiit's ūü§™ comedy time!!! ūü§™ Round up your friends and head to Queeny's on Sunday, May 31 at 7:30pm for the third installment of Taking Up Space with Sarah LaTour! This now monthly event (every last Sunday) features an eclectic bunch of storytellers, improv artists, hilarious queers, and whatever else they want to be during these highly entertaining evenings!

This month we'll be joined by your host sarahalatour along with fariha_laughs, caty.collins, victoria.queteapie, okswade, and rahkiej!

events.title: ūüĆą Craft Night
events.description:
Calling all crafty girls!!! Our friends at forthegirlsofraleigh are hosting a Craft Night at our place this Wednesday, May 13 from 6-9pm for any and all crafty sapphics!

Join us if you're the owner of a half-finished scrapbook, a compulsive sticker collector, or someone who just needs something to do with their hands. ‚úāÔłŹ‚ú® Bring your own project or use what's on hand to make something new ‚Äď this is a relaxing, casual time!

See forthegirlsofraleigh for details! *Sliding Scale/Donation Based Entry*

events.title: ūüĆą May Day Demonstration
events.description:
We‚Äôll be closed from 5‚Äď6pm Friday so our team can participate in the May Day demonstration if they choose.

We support efforts that advocate for fair opportunity, preserving democracy, ice reform, and a stronger, more equitable community.

We‚Äôll reopen at 6pm and continue regular service.

events.title: ūüĆą Dancing With The Carolina Stars: Celebration
events.description:
Dancing with the Stars ‚Äď DURHAM edition! ūüĆü

Michelle is officially competing in carolinatheatredurham‚Äôs Dancing With The Carolina Stars: Celebration on May 20! She‚Äôll be on stage with her dance partner Alyssa ascho3 supporting the theater‚Äôs mission during their biggest fundraising night of the year. 

While Michelle loves a dance party, Alyssa brings choreography to the table as one half of A+A Dance Company aandadanceco and co-director and co-producer for RECITAL recital_durham. Vote for this powerhouse team via donation to the Carolina Theatre! Link via carolinatheatredurham ūüíĖ

Every ticket sold and donation/vote given supports the Carolina Theatre, one of Durham‚Äôs most beloved institutions that provides a platform that allows artists to entertain and express through the creation of emotional and educational experiences that reshape hearts and minds.

Come support the arts & all the folks dancing!

events.title: ūüĆą Taking Up Space with Sarah LaTour
events.description:
Comedy ‚Äď now every last Sunday!!! You all loved March's show with the multitalented Sarah LaTour so much that we said, hey, why don't we keep doing this?!

So join us on Sunday, April 26 at 7:30pm for Taking Up Space with Sarah LaTour ‚Äď a queer, POC, women, and gender expansive led night of comedy, storytelling, and fun. 

Featuringggg: sarahalatour, nik.narain, ebonysingsfunny, caty.collins, okswade, rahkiej, fariha_laughs ūüéČ

events.title: ūüĆą Fernet Celebration - Merchants of the Know Award
events.description:
Fernet celebration this Wednesday 4/1!!! 3-5pm!

Come on out as our friends at Fernet award us/you for being loyal Fernet imbibers. This means all you industry folk, Fernet fans, and those with a fun free 2 hours 3-5pm on a week day are INVITED!

We are being awarded with the Merchants of the Know award, honoring top sellers and those with long term commitment to pouring Fernet at their bars. 

There will be a trophy, cupcakes, Fernet swag, and SPECIALTY cocktail menu featuring Fernet and Borghetti. Menu on both Tues and Weds!

events.title: ūüĆą St. Patrick's Day Celebration

events.title: ūüĆą WLW Stand Up Comedy Night
events.description:
Comedy @ Queeny's!!! Our very own Sarah LaTour is hosting a WLW Stand Up Comedy Night featuring four hyper talented (and might I state the obvious: hilarious) women who bring a little bit of everything to the show - sketch, improv, stand up, sapphic storytelling, and more. Here they are: sarahalatour  caty.collins, rahkiej, _beeoriginal, fariha_laughs ūüéČ

Join us next Sunday, March 22 at 7:30pm! Come for some laughs, good company, gay humor, new cocktail menu (!!!), and a good time.

events.title: ūüĆą Black Genius Fest
events.description:
Happy to be a community partner for #BlackGeniusFest villageofwisdom! ūüĒ• 

Join our friends on Parrish St. today to celebrate Black history, culture, creativity, and entrepreneurial energy! 

Then swing by for lunch for our partner special ‚Äď in honor of the event, we've zhuzhed up our baked mac n cheese, adding collards and candied bacon on top. It's a banger.

‚ÄúThis exhibition brings together the timeless wisdom of Aesop‚Äôs Fables and beloved children‚Äôs animal stories, reimagined through the intricate visual language of Gond-inspired art,‚ÄĚ Sampada says. ‚ÄúRooted in the indigenous traditions of central India, Gond art is known for its rhythmic lines, detailed patterns, and deep connection to nature and storytelling.‚ÄĚ

events.org: Chapel Hill Arts - Small Town Pride
events.description:
Save the date for the annual Chapel Hill-Carrboro Juneteenth Celebration!

On Friday, June 19 from 4‚Äď8pm, come to Hargraves Community Center to enjoy live music and line dancing, shop local vendors and food trucks, and celebrate Juneteenth with your neighbors.

Juneteenth, also known as Emancipation Day, commemorates the emancipation of enslaved people in the United States. The Towns of Chapel Hill and Carrboro proclaimed the observance and commemoration of Juneteenth on June 19, 2020 and encourage residents and employers to recognize the holiday.

events.org: Chapel Hill Arts - Small Town Pride
events.description:
Artist call! ūüĖľÔłŹ It‚Äôs time for our next round of individual and group exhibitions at chapelhillpubliclibrary and chapelhillgov Town Hall.

We seek vibrant, high-quality artwork in a variety of media by a wide range of local artists. Whether this is your first or 50th exhibit, we welcome you to apply and share your work with our community. 

Public Spaces is an indoor exhibition series in Chapel Hill. Exhibits rotate every two months and are free for the public to visit. Swipe ‚ě°ÔłŹ to see some of our recent artists and curators.

What‚Äôs in it for you?
ūüĎČ $300 stipend for lead artist/curator
ūüĎČ Two-month exhibition
ūüĎČ Your work on display at a high-traffic exhibition space in Chapel Hill
ūüĎČ Artwork may be for sale ‚ÄĒ we take no commission!

Eligibility: Triangle-area artists, artist collectives, and curators ages 18+, who create original art that can be hung on walls indoors

events.org: Chapel Hill Arts - Small Town Pride
events.description:
Have you seen our latest exhibition by Voon Wong (artvw)? 

Exploring everyday interactions and drawing from his own personal experiences, Voon‚Äôs watercolors are rooted in memory, culture, and humanity. 
 
Voon discovered his passion for art in high school and studied at an art college in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, before earning a master‚Äôs degree from academy_of_art. His early watercolor paintings feature serene landscapes and local fishing boats, reflecting his connection to coastal life. Today, he focuses on capturing human experiences. In 2025, he was honored with Signature Member status in both the nwsgallery and the watercolorsocietync.

What are dreams and what do they mean? How do emotions shape our lives?
 
These are questions that artist brook.ramsey tries to answer in the latest exhibition at chapelhillgov Town Hall, called ‚ÄúBeyond the Surface.‚ÄĚ
 
‚ÄúThis series is a story about how our lives are transformed by what we choose to take away from our experiences,‚ÄĚ Brook says. ‚ÄúIt is also about the transformative power that honoring our emotions has in our lives.‚ÄĚ
 
Come visit (it‚Äôs free!):
ūüĒĻ On display until April 9
ūüĒĻ 405 MLK Jr. Blvd, Chapel Hill, NC
ūüĒĻ Hours: Mon-Fri, 8:30am-5pm
 Month-long Pride celebration hosted by Chapel Hill Community Arts & Culture.
